message:
feat(engine): add Reflect.apply/construct API and reject keyword module import bindings

Object model — Reflect.apply() and Reflect.construct() (ES2020 §26.1):
- Implement Reflect.apply(target, thisArgument, argumentsList) which validates
  that the target is callable (or a Proxy) and returns a ReflectApplyRequest
  struct for the interpreter to dispatch the actual call
- Implement Reflect.construct(target, argumentsList, newTarget?) which validates
  both target and optional newTarget are constructors, defaulting newTarget to
  target when omitted, and returns a ReflectConstructRequest
- Add ReflectApplyRequest and ReflectConstructRequest data types with full
  serde support for the interpreter integration contract
- Both methods handle Proxy targets by deferring trap resolution to the
  interpreter layer, maintaining the separation between object model
  validation and execution dispatch

Parser — keyword rejection in module binding positions:
- Introduce is_module_binding_identifier() which validates that an identifier
  is not a reserved keyword before accepting it as a module binding target
- Add is_disallowed_module_binding_name() covering all ES2020 reserved words
  plus strict-mode-only reserved words (implements, interface, let, package,
  private, protected, public, static, yield)
- Replace bare is_identifier() calls in parse_import_binding_clause(),
  parse_namespace_import_binding(), and is_named_import_clause() with the
  new module-binding-aware variant
- Named import specifiers now correctly allow keywords as the source (export
  name) but reject them as the local binding target (e.g. `import { x as for }`
  now fails)
- Update error message to "default import binding must be a non-keyword
  identifier" for clearer diagnostics
- Add integration test: parser_rejects_keyword_module_import_bindings verifies
  `import { run as for } from "pkg"` is rejected with UnsupportedSyntax

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
